topic: What exists in JASON-OS that needs classification for bidirectional sync with SoNash?

consumer_hints.piece_2_schema_design: Use D13 MVP schema (12 fields) as starting point. Add data_contracts[] as 13th field for composite-type entries. Decide source_scope vs runtime_scope split before finalizing. Canonical-memory promotion workflow is most acute gap requiring schema support.
consumer_hints.piece_1b_sonash_scan: Apply all Section 7 learnings before dispatching SoNash agents. Key adjustments: byte-weighted skill splits, 4 memory agents, 2-3 hook agents, team-file HTML-comment parser, add lineage+deferred_sections+module_system+originSessionId+data_contracts to Wave 1 JSONL spec before dispatch.
consumer_hints.schema_mvp: 12 fields: name, path, type, scope, portability, status, purpose, dependencies, lineage, supersedes, superseded_by, sanitize_fields, notes. These 12 address every primary sync decision across all unit types.
consumer_hints.critical_findings: 5 critical findings in Section 5: content bleed, 50-file canonical gap, source_scope/runtime_scope split, data_contracts omission, cache-dir naming convention.
consumer_hints.sonash_learnings: Section 7 of RESEARCH_OUTPUT.md is the primary input to piece-1b dispatch planning. Contains 6 subsections: agent sizing, schema adjustments, file-type observations, classification heuristics, dependency extraction techniques, SoNash-specific expectations.

upstream_dependencies: research/file-registry-portability-graph (R-frpg) — scope enum + Option D are binding inputs to Piece 2
research/jason-os-mvp — PORT_ANALYSIS.md is the direct ancestor of the file-registry concept

scan_scope_gaps: scripts/skills/ directory (per-skill self-audit scripts not inventoried)
docs/ directory (referenced by skill-creator but not in D9 scope)
